extraction.article_title: Adobe fixes actively exploited Acrobat Reader flaw CVE-2026-34621

ttps.description: Exploitation for Client Execution via a malicious PDF that triggers code execution in Adobe Acrobat Reader when the document is opened or previewed.
User Execution through opening a weaponized document delivered by phishing or download.

attack_chain: Victim receives or downloads a malicious PDF
PDF is opened in Adobe Acrobat Reader or a preview-enabled handler
The crafted document triggers the vulnerable parsing/rendering/code path
Arbitrary code execution occurs in the context of the user
proposed_phase_updates: Add a document-based client-side exploitation phase for weaponized PDF delivery and execution.

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A malformed PDF object stream, font table, or annotation structure can trigger a memory corruption or parser bug in the document viewer, leading to code execution when the file is rendered.
proposed_kb_patterns.payload:
%PDF-1.7
1 0 obj
<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>
endobj
2 0 obj
<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>
endobj
3 0 obj
<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /Resources << /Font << /F1 4 0 R >> >> /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] /Contents 5 0 R >>
endobj
4 0 obj
<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>
endobj
5 0 obj
<< /Length 120 >>
stream
BT /F1 12 Tf 72 720 Td (AAAA...<very long repeated string>...AAAA) Tj ET
endstream
endobj
xref
0 6
0000000000 65535 f 
trailer
<< /Root 1 0 R >>
startxref
0
%%EOF
detect: EDR alerts for Acrobat/Reader spawning child processes
unexpected heap corruption or access violation logs
malicious PDF flagged by sandbox detonation
